steps.title: Find the parallel structure
steps.content: The phrase “Compared with that of a highly concentrated market” establishes the first side of a comparison. Here, “that” refers to “HHI score”—so the comparison is between two scores. In such parallel comparisons, both sides must have matching structure.

steps.title: Apply the parallel structure
steps.content:
Choice B—“the HHI score of a less concentrated market will be much lower”—completes the comparison with an HHI score as the subject, creating the required score-to-score structure.

Choices A, C, and D either shift to the market as subject (A, D) or introduce a conditional that breaks the direct comparison (C).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
